candidates.reason: Exact agency identity. Both records identify the New York City Police Department / NYPD; the alias is a newer police-department extraction while the target is the broader NYPD reference already used across bus-priority sources.

evidence_refs: 14th_street_busway#p001_b0001 quote: NYPD traffic agents may also issue summonses
14th_street_busway_brochure#p001_c0030 quote: Curb regulations are enforced by NYPD traffic agents

candidates.reason: Exact agency identity. The alias record explicitly says New York City Transit Authority is MTA New York City Transit; the target is the richer NYCT agency reference.

evidence_refs: meeting_doc_131666#p003_c0012 quote: New York City Transit Authority ("MTA New York City Transit")

do_not_merge.reason: Bureau versus parent agency. NYPD's Transit Bureau is a bureau within the New York City Police Department, not the same canonical entity as NYPD itself.

evidence_refs: meeting_doc_100361#p001_c0003 quote: The report is submitted by NYPD's Transit Bureau
meeting_doc_102826#p001_c0003 quote: The report is submitted by NYPD's Transit Bureau
14th_street_busway#p001_b0001 quote: NYPD traffic agents may also issue summonses
14th_street_busway_brochure#p001_c0030 quote: Curb regulations are enforced by NYPD traffic agents

do_not_merge.reason: Person versus agency. Alexander Lindsay is a person affiliated with the MTA Police Department; the MTA Police Department is the agency.

evidence.role: Commanding Officer of Internal Affairs, Designated Employer Representative (DER) for MTA Police Department

evidence_refs: meeting_doc_105786#p002_c0029 quote: MTA Police will highlight the significant police activities

do_not_merge.reason: Different operating agencies. The Long Island Rail Road Company / MTA Long Island Rail Road is distinct from New York City Transit Authority / NYCT; shared LIRR aliases on the NYCT record are contamination.

evidence.entity_name: The Long Island Rail Road Company

evidence_refs: meeting_doc_128981#p033_c0004 quote: Due to New York City Transit Authority

do_not_merge.reason: Different railroad agencies. MTA Long Island Rail Road and MTA Metro-North Railroad are sibling agencies, not the same entity; shared LIRR aliases on the Metro-North record are contamination.

evidence.entity_name: The Long Island Rail Road Company
evidence_refs: meeting_doc_104766#p001_c0001 quote: Long Island Rail Road
meeting_doc_131536#p001_c0007 quote: AGENCY: MTA Metro-North Railroad ("MNR")

do_not_merge.reason: Subsidiary agency versus parent authority. NYCT is an operating agency under MTA; it should not merge into the Metropolitan Transportation Authority parent record.

evidence_refs: meeting_doc_128981#p033_c0004 quote: Due to New York City Transit Authority

do_not_merge.reason: Agency versus board committee. The MTA Long Island Rail Road agency is distinct from the Long Island Rail Road Committee.

evidence.entity_name: The Long Island Rail Road Company

do_not_merge.reason: Facility versus operating company. Grand Central Madison is a station/facility; Grand Central Madison Operating Company is an MTA agency/company responsible for the LIRR-operating portion, not the station itself.

evidence_refs: meeting_doc_121001#p001_c0007 quote: Grand Central Madison Operating Company (GCMOC)

do_not_merge.reason: Person versus agency. Janno Lieber is an individual MTA chair/CEO, while Metropolitan Transportation Authority is the agency; shared Janno Lieber aliases on the MTA record are person-field contamination.
